name: Creation of Resources in Critical Namespaces
notes: This rule triggers when resources are created under critical namespaces, such as kube-system or kube-public. The namespace kube-system should only be used by objects created from Kubernetes system. The namespace kube-public is readable by all users, which must be used with caution.
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers

when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Network Peering Success, Authentication.Access Key Creation Success, Authentication.Role Creation Success, Authentication.Role Assignment Success, Audit.Machine Creation Success, System.Task Creation Success, System.Cron Successful, Audit.Create Activity Succeeded, Audit.Configure Activity Succeeded
when the event matches Namespace (custom) is any of [kube-system or kube-public]
TD_list: APPLY Creation of Resources in Critical Namespaces on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Containers

AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.Network Peering Success, Authentication.Access Key Creation Success, Authentication.Role Creation Success, Authentication.Role Assignment Success, Audit.Machine Creation Success, System.Task Creation Success, System.Cron Successful, Audit.Create Activity Succeeded, Audit.Configure Activity Succeeded
AND when the event matches Namespace (custom) is any of [kube-system or kube-public]
RED_list: Event name: Creation of Resources in Critical Namespaces
Event description: Detects when multiple sensitive resources are created.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Username
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
